phones.name: voiceless bilabial stop
phones.description: Lips closed, then released with no vocal-fold vibration. English 'p' in 'pin'.

phones.name: voiced bilabial stop
phones.description: Lips closed, released with voicing. English 'b' in 'bin'.

phones.name: voiceless alveolar stop
phones.description: Tongue tip against the alveolar ridge, released without voicing. English 't' in 'top'.

phones.name: voiced alveolar stop
phones.description: Tongue tip against the alveolar ridge, released with voicing. English 'd' in 'dog'.

phones.name: voiceless retroflex stop
phones.description: Tongue tip curled back to the post-alveolar region. Telugu and Hindi 'ṭ'.

phones.name: voiced retroflex stop
phones.description: Tongue tip curled back, with voicing. Telugu and Hindi 'ḍ'.

phones.name: voiceless palatal stop
phones.description: Body of the tongue against the hard palate, released without voicing. Hungarian 'ty'.

phones.name: voiced palatal stop
phones.description: Body of the tongue against the hard palate, with voicing. Hungarian 'gy'.

phones.name: voiceless velar stop
phones.description: Back of the tongue against the soft palate, released without voicing. English 'k' in 'kit'.

phones.name: voiced velar stop
phones.description: Back of the tongue against the soft palate, with voicing. English 'g' in 'go'.

phones.name: voiceless uvular stop
phones.description: Back of the tongue against the uvula. Arabic 'ق' (qaf).

phones.name: glottal stop
phones.description: Vocal folds closed and abruptly released. The catch in 'uh-oh'; Arabic 'ء' (hamza).

phones.name: bilabial nasal
phones.description: Lips closed, air flows through the nose. English 'm' in 'man'.

phones.name: alveolar nasal
phones.description: Tongue against the alveolar ridge, air flows through the nose. English 'n' in 'no'.

phones.name: retroflex nasal
phones.description: Tongue tip curled back, air through the nose. Telugu 'ṇ'.

phones.name: palatal nasal
phones.description: Body of the tongue at the palate, air through the nose. Spanish 'ñ', French 'gn'.

phones.name: velar nasal
phones.description: Back of the tongue at the soft palate, air through the nose. English 'ng' in 'sing'.

phones.name: voiceless labiodental fricative
phones.description: Upper teeth on lower lip, air flows through. English 'f' in 'fan'.

phones.name: voiced labiodental fricative
phones.description: Upper teeth on lower lip, with voicing. English 'v' in 'van'.

phones.name: voiceless dental fricative
phones.description: Tongue tip lightly behind the upper teeth; air flows through. English 'th' in 'thin'; Arabic 'ث'.

phones.name: voiced dental fricative
phones.description: Tongue tip lightly behind the upper teeth, with voicing. English 'th' in 'this'; Arabic 'ذ'.

phones.name: voiceless alveolar fricative
phones.description: Tongue near the alveolar ridge, air channeled through a groove. English 's' in 'sit'.

phones.name: voiced alveolar fricative
phones.description: Tongue near the alveolar ridge, with voicing. English 'z' in 'zoo'.

phones.name: voiceless postalveolar fricative
phones.description: Tongue blade slightly behind the alveolar ridge, lips often rounded. English 'sh' in 'ship'.

phones.name: voiced postalveolar fricative
phones.description: Tongue blade behind the alveolar ridge, with voicing. English 's' in 'measure'.

phones.name: voiceless retroflex fricative
phones.description: Tongue tip curled back, air channeled through. Telugu 'ష'; Mandarin 'sh'.

phones.name: voiceless velar fricative
phones.description: Back of the tongue near the soft palate, air friction without voicing. German 'ch' in 'Bach'; Arabic 'خ'.

phones.name: voiced velar fricative
phones.description: Back of the tongue near the soft palate, with voicing. Arabic 'غ'; Greek 'γ' before back vowels.

phones.name: voiceless pharyngeal fricative
phones.description: Constriction in the pharynx, voiceless. Arabic 'ح'.

phones.name: voiced pharyngeal fricative
phones.description: Constriction in the pharynx, with voicing. Arabic 'ع' (ʿayn).

phones.name: voiceless glottal fricative
phones.description: Air friction at the glottis, no oral constriction. English 'h' in 'hat'; Arabic 'ه'.

phones.name: alveolar tap
phones.description: A single quick tap of the tongue tip against the alveolar ridge. Spanish single 'r'; American English 'tt' in 'butter'.

phones.name: alveolar trill
phones.description: Multiple rapid taps of the tongue against the alveolar ridge. Spanish double 'rr'; Arabic 'ر'.

phones.name: alveolar lateral approximant
phones.description: Tongue at the alveolar ridge, air flows around the sides. English 'l' in 'lip'.

phones.name: retroflex lateral approximant
phones.description: Tongue tip curled back, air flows around the sides. Telugu 'ళ'; Tamil 'ள'.

phones.name: palatal approximant
phones.description: Body of the tongue close to the palate without friction. English 'y' in 'yes'.

phones.name: labio-velar approximant
phones.description: Lips rounded and back of the tongue raised. English 'w' in 'wet'.

phones.name: voiceless postalveolar affricate
phones.description: A 't' released directly into 'ʃ'. English 'ch' in 'church'; Arabic dialect 'چ'.

phones.name: voiced postalveolar affricate
phones.description: A 'd' released directly into 'ʒ'. English 'j' in 'judge'; Arabic 'ج' (in many dialects).

phones.name: voiceless alveolar affricate
phones.description: A 't' released directly into 's'. German 'z' in 'Zeit'; Italian 'zz' in 'pizza'.

phones.name: voiced alveolar affricate
phones.description: A 'd' released directly into 'z'. Italian 'zz' in 'mezzo'.

phones.name: close front unrounded vowel
phones.description: Tongue high and forward, lips spread. English 'ee' in 'see'; Arabic 'ـِ' long.

phones.name: near-close near-front unrounded vowel
phones.description: Slightly lower and more central than 'i'. English 'i' in 'sit'.

phones.name: close-mid front unrounded vowel
phones.description: Tongue forward, mid-high, lips spread. Spanish 'e' in 'mesa'.

phones.name: open-mid front unrounded vowel
phones.description: Tongue forward, mid-low, lips spread. English 'e' in 'bed'.

phones.name: near-open front unrounded vowel
phones.description: Tongue forward, low, mouth open. English 'a' in 'cat'.

phones.name: open front unrounded vowel
phones.description: Tongue forward and low, mouth fully open. Spanish 'a' in 'casa'.

phones.name: close central unrounded vowel
phones.description: Tongue high and centered. Russian 'ы'; Romanian 'î'.

phones.name: mid central vowel
phones.description: Tongue at rest in the center of the mouth. English unstressed 'a' in 'about'.

phones.name: open back unrounded vowel
phones.description: Tongue back and low, lips relaxed. English 'a' in American 'father'.

phones.name: open-mid back rounded vowel
phones.description: Tongue back, mid-low, lips rounded. English 'aw' in 'thought' (in many accents).

phones.name: close-mid back rounded vowel
phones.description: Tongue back, mid-high, lips rounded. Spanish 'o' in 'lobo'.

phones.name: near-close near-back rounded vowel
phones.description: Slightly lower and more central than 'u'. English 'oo' in 'foot'.

phones.name: close back rounded vowel
phones.description: Tongue high and back, lips rounded. Spanish 'u' in 'luna'.

phones.name: close front rounded vowel
phones.description: Tongue high and forward, lips rounded. French 'u' in 'tu'; German 'ü'.

phones.name: close-mid front rounded vowel
phones.description: Tongue forward and mid-high, lips rounded. French 'eu' in 'peu'; German 'ö'.

phones.name: close front unrounded long vowel
phones.description: Long version of 'i'. Arabic 'ـِي'; Telugu 'ీ'.

phones.name: open front unrounded long vowel
phones.description: Long version of 'a'. Arabic 'ـَا'; Telugu 'ా'.

phones.name: close back rounded long vowel
phones.description: Long version of 'u'. Arabic 'ـُو'; Telugu 'ూ'.

phones.name: close-mid front unrounded long vowel
phones.description: Long version of 'e'. Telugu 'ే'.

phones.name: close-mid back rounded long vowel
phones.description: Long version of 'o'. Telugu 'ో'.
